commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch routes unresumable WebChat restart-recovery notices into the session transcript and adds focused regression tests for WebChat transcript delivery and append failure handling.

PR surface: Source +96, Tests +212. Total +308 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main filters WebChat out of message.action, and the PR body supplies live Gateway restart plus chat.history output; I did not rerun the live scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow mechanical blocker on the PR branch that automation can repair without a product decision.

target.title: fix(agents): show WebChat the restart recovery notice via the session transcript
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87808.

When the Gateway restarts mid-turn during a WebChat direct chat (bundled Codex harness), the interrupted turn is marked unresumable and the user is supposed to receive a recovery notice. But that notice is delivered via `message.action` (an outbound channel send), and WebChat is an internal-only channel that rejects `message.action`. So `resolveRestartRecoveryDeliveryContext` returns `undefined` (WebChat fails `isDeliverableMessageChannel`), the notice is silently dropped, and on reconnect the turn just vanishes — no assistant reply, no interruption notice. The user has to send another message to discover what happened.

## What changed

**`src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts`** — in `sendUnresumableSessionNotice`, detect the WebChat (internal) channel from the raw delivery channel *before* the deliverable-channel filter. For WebChat, persist the recovery notice to the session transcript via `appendInjectedAssistantMessageToTranscript` (a gateway-injected assistant message written to disk + a live transcript update) instead of an outbound `message.action`, so a reconnecting WebChat client renders the terminal notice. Non-WebChat channels keep the existing `message.action` delivery.

**`src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`** — regression test: an unresumable WebChat session no longer issues an outbound `message.action`, and the recovery notice is persisted to the transcript (read back from disk).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** An interrupted WebChat turn now leaves a visible terminal notice in the session transcript instead of silently vanishing on reconnect. The notice is persisted to disk so a reconnecting WebChat client renders it; non-WebChat channels keep their outbound delivery.

**Real environment tested:** Local worktree of `origin/main` at base `118060157d`, Node v22.22.0. Drove the real `recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions` → `sendUnresumableSessionNotice` path via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`, against a real on-disk session store + transcript (`sessions.json` + `main-session.jsonl`). The notice is read back from the real transcript file.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts -t "WebChat transcript"
# Baseline measured by git-stashing the recovery fix and re-running the same test.
```

**Evidence after fix:** With the fix stashed (origin behavior), the WebChat session's transcript never gets the notice and the test fails:
```
src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts (21 tests | 1 failed | 20 skipped)
AssertionError: expected '{"message":{"role":"user",...}}' to contain "couldn't safely resume"
```
With the fix applied, the notice is persisted and the full suite passes:
```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  21 passed (21)
```

**Observed result after fix:** The unresumable WebChat session no longer issues an outbound `message.action` (WebChat would reject it); instead the recovery notice ("…couldn't safely resume…") is written to the session transcript on disk, where a reconnecting WebChat client reads it. Session status is still marked `failed`.

**Live end-to-end proof (real Gateway restart + real model turn + reconnect):** `scripts/repro/webchat-restart-recovery-notice-live-proof.mts` spawns a real isolated Gateway instance backed by a real `zte/step3p5-flash` model, sends a real WebChat `chat.send` turn, then **SIGKILLs the Gateway mid-turn** — a graceful SIGTERM would release the transcript lock, so only a hard crash leaves the stale lock restart-recovery keys on. Restarting over the same state dir runs startup restart-recovery, whose own gateway logs show the causal chain:
```
[gateway] removed stale session lock: <sessionId>.jsonl.lock (dead-pid)
[main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) from stale transcript locks
[main-session-restart-recovery] persisted interrupted main session recovery notice to transcript: agent:main:main (transcript tail is not resumable)
[main-session-restart-recovery] main-session restart recovery complete: recovered=0 failed=1 skipped=0
```
On reconnect, `chat.history` (the WebChat reconnect read-path) returns the recovery notice as the assistant message, with the session now `status: failed` / `abortedLastRun: true` / `deliveryContext.channel: webchat`:
```
{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I was interrupted by a gateway restart and couldn't safely resume the previous turn. Please send that last request again and I'll pick it up cleanly."}]}
```
Reproduced 3× locally, all green. (The local repro harness reads provider config to drive a real model turn and writes temp artifacts, so — per review — it is not committed to the branch; the gateway restart-recovery logs and the reconnect `chat.history` output above are the captured evidence.)

**What was not tested:** No rendered browser/Control-UI screenshot — the reconnect is verified through the `chat.history` API (the exact read-path the WebChat client uses), not a UI capture.

## Validation (supplemental)

- `main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts` full suite → 21 passed (before fix: WebChat case fails as shown above)
- `tsgo:core:test` → no new errors in changed files; `oxlint` → clean
